title: Bennettia trinervia
taxonomy: Bennettia trinervia is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The classification of Bennettia trinervia places it within the order Magnoliales, which is an ancient group of flowering plants. This classification indicates that Bennettia trinervia is an ancient and relatively primitive species within the Annonaceae family. Further research is needed to fully understand the classification and evolutionary history of Bennettia trinervia.

This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
